data.title: Wall Street's Most Accurate Analysts Weigh In On 3 Industrials Stocks With Over 3% Dividend Yields
data.description:
During times of turbulence and uncertainty in the markets, many investors turn to dividend-yielding stocks. These are often companies that have high free cash flows and reward shareholders with a high dividend payout.

Below are the ratings of the most accurate analysts for three high-yielding stocks in the industrials sector.

Watsco Inc (NYSE:WSO)

Korn Ferry (NYSE:KFY)

data.title: What's Going On With CBRE Group Stock Thursday?
data.description:
The transaction highlights CBRE’s growing push into real assets and critical infrastructure, leveraging its investment platform to expand beyond traditional real estate into resource-linked sectors.

CBRE Investment Management, which operates independently but draws on CBRE’s global data, sourcing, and market insights, is supporting CRR’s first strategic move into forestry—broadening its exposure across Canada’s resource industries.

The deal underscores CBRE’s strategy to deploy capital into infrastructure assets that generate long-term, stable returns while supporting industrial and resource supply chains.

Technical Analysis

CBRE is currently trading within its 52-week range, reflecting a solid performance over the past year with a return of 25.83%. The stock is trading 7.5% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA), suggesting a bullish short-term trend, while it is 3.8% below its 100-day SMA, indicating some resistance in the intermediate term.

The relative strength index (RSI) is at 62.24, which suggests that the stock is in neutral territory, indicating balanced momentum. This positioning implies that while there is potential for upward movement, traders should watch for any signs of overbought conditions.

  • Key Resistance: $153.00 — A level where selling pressure may emerge.
  • Key Support: $143.00 — A potential area for buyers to step in.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

The countdown is on: CBRE Group is set to report earnings on April 23, 2026 (confirmed).

  • EPS Estimate: $1.13 (Up from 86 cents)
  • Revenue Estimate: $10.13 Billion (Up from $8.91 Billion)
  • Valuation: P/E of 38.3x (Indicates premium valuation)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $176.67. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Barclays: Overweight (Raises Target to $175.00) (April 14)
  • Barclays: Overweight (Lowers Target to $174.00) (March 13)
  • UBS: Upgraded to Buy (Raises Target to $185.00) (February 23)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for CBRE Group, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value Rank: 20.13 — Indicates a weak value proposition relative to peers.
  • Growth Rank: 11.08 — Suggests limited growth potential in the current environment.
  • Quality Rank: 74.52 — Reflects strong operational performance and stability.
  • Momentum Rank: 34.36 — Indicates moderate momentum, suggesting a cautious outlook.

The Verdict: CBRE Group’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a mixed profile, with strong quality metrics but weak value and growth scores, suggesting a focus on operational stability amidst broader market challenges.

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because CBRE carries significant weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

Price Action

CBRE Stock Price Activity: CBRE Group shares gained 0.28% to close at $147.48 on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Gevo Pulls DOE Loan Bid, Stock Slides As Company Seeks New Funding
data.description:
Department of Energy's Office of Energy Dominance Financing for its ATJ-30 project.

Financing Shift

The company said the DOE required the project to support enhanced oil recovery, which is not yet commercially viable at scale in the project area.

Gevo is pursuing alternative financing options better aligned with its strategy, aiming to accelerate execution and improve returns.

The withdrawal allows the company to reapply for financing at a later date if it chooses.

Project Continuity

Gevo had been working to transfer a prior conditional DOE loan guarantee—originally tied to its ATJ-60 sustainable aviation fuel project in South Dakota—to the ATJ-30 project at its Gevo North Dakota facility.

The company will continue advancing ATJ-30 at the site, which includes low-carbon ethanol production and carbon capture and sequestration capabilities.

CEO Commentary

CEO Paul Bloom said, "Over the past year, we have substantially derisked our position in low-carbon ethanol required for our ATJ and we believe we are in a better position to secure alternative financing that allows us to pursue the most accretive business case in alignment with company strategy and timelines."

He added that the North Dakota facility is already generating cash and can support EBITDA growth. It also serves as a foundation for expanding Gevo's sustainable fuels business.

Short Interest Declines

Short interest in Gevo fell to 30.33 million shares from 31.53 million in the latest reporting period, representing 12.64% of its public float.

Based on an average daily trading volume of 4.60 million shares, it would take about 6.6 days for short sellers to cover their positions.

Technical Analysis

Gevo is currently trading 19% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 12.4% below its 50-day SMA, suggesting bearish short-term momentum.

The stock is also 7% below its 200-day SMA, indicating a longer-term downtrend as it struggles to regain upward traction.

The relative strength index (RSI) is at 42.17, suggesting neutral momentum and no strong buying or selling pressure.

  • Key Resistance: $2.00 — A level where selling pressure has historically emerged.
  • Key Support: $1.50 — A critical level that could indicate a reversal if tested.

Gevo's 12-month performance shows a return of 70.94%, reflecting strong growth over the past year despite recent challenges.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Gevo is slated to provide its next financial update on May 12, 2026 (estimated).

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 2 cents (up from a loss of 9 cents)
  • Revenue Estimate: $44.94 million (up from $29.11 million)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Strong Buy rating with an average price target of $4.69. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Northland Capital Markets: Outperform (Maintains Target to $3.00) (Dec. 31, 2025)
  • UBS: Neutral (Raises Target to $2.25) (Sept. 17, 2025)
  • HC Wainwright & Co.: Buy (Maintains Target to $14.00) (May 30, 2025)

GEVO Stock Price Activity: Gevo shares were down 16.00% at $1.68 during premarket trading on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: What's Going On With Transocean Stock Today?
data.description:
This news follows a series of significant backlog additions totaling approximately $1.6 billion since early April, which may be boosting investor confidence as broader markets edged higher.

Transocean’s recent contract for the Deepwater Asgard is part of an estimated 390-day campaign expected to commence in the fourth quarter of 2026.

This contract is anticipated to contribute significantly to the company’s backlog, highlighting its ongoing operational strength in the offshore drilling sector.

The broader market saw gains on Wednesday, with the Energy sector rising 0.09%. RIG’s upward movement aligns with this trend, suggesting that the stock is benefiting from overall positive market sentiment.

Transocean recently secured a 1,156-day contract extension for its Deepwater Corcovado rig with Petrobras, which will keep the rig active through November 2030.

The firm is an international provider of offshore contract drilling services for oil and gas wells. This extension is projected to contribute around $445 million to the company’s backlog, enhancing its revenue outlook.

Technical Analysis

Transocean is currently trading within a strong technical setup, positioned 5.3% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 1.2% below its 50-day SMA, indicating a short-term bearish trend. However, the stock is trading 17.2% above its 100-day SMA and 45.4% above its 200-day SMA, suggesting a robust long-term bullish momentum.

The relative strength index (RSI) is at 43.85, indicating a neutral momentum, which suggests that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold at this time. This positioning allows for potential upward movement if buying pressure increases.

  • Key Resistance: $7.00 — A significant level where selling pressure may emerge.
  • Key Support: $6.00 — A critical level that could indicate a reversal if tested.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

The countdown is on: Transocean is set to report earnings on April 27, 2026 (estimated). Analysts are closely watching the upcoming earnings call, with expectations for earnings per share of 7 cents, up from a loss of 10 cents in the same quarter last year, and revenue estimates of $1.02 billion, up from $906 million.

  • EPS Estimate: 7 cents (Up from a loss of 10 cents)
  • Revenue Estimate: $1.02 billion (Up from $906 million)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold Rating with an average price target of $7.10. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Morgan Stanley: Equal-Weight (Raises Target to $7.00) (April 15)
  • Susquehanna: Positive (Raises Target to $8.00) (April 7)
  • Morgan Stanley: Equal-Weight (Raises Target to $5.00) (Feb. 23)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Transocean, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Growth: Strong (Score: 1.82) — Indicates solid growth potential.
  • Momentum: Bullish (Score: 96.39) — Stock is outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Transocean’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a growth-heavy profile with strong momentum, suggesting that the stock is well-positioned for continued performance in the market.

RIG Price Action: Transocean shares were up 0.97% at $6.20 during premarket trading on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock
